data.title: Some Republican-led states are slowly expanding paid parental leave programs, historically resistant to it.
data.description: Lawmakers in various states, particularly in Republican-led ones that have traditionally opposed paid leave policies, are gradually increasing access to paid parental leave programs. This shift indicates a growing recognition of the importance of supporting new parents in the workforce.

data.title: Elon Musk offered $97.4 billion for OpenAI's parent company that Zuckerberg declined, leading to legal battles.ведитеVyome is considering selling Livechain. Mercuries Life Insurance explores sale. GTCR $627M buyout faces antitrust scrutiny. Cenovus to purchase MEG Energy.
data.description: Elon Musk made a $97.4 billion offer to buy the parent company of ChatGPT, but Mark Zuckerberg and Meta Platforms did not sign a letter of intent. OpenAI, led by CEO Sam Altman, is now in a legal battle with Meta over the takeover bid. Vyome is reviewing options for its subsidiary Livechain, acquired in 2021, including launching a new business or selling it outright. Mercuries Life Insurance in Taiwan is considering a potential sale or stake sale. Peraso Inc. is exploring strategic options, but potential acquirer Mobix Labs wants direct access to the board. GTCR's $627 million buyout of Surmodics Inc. is under antitrust scrutiny. Cenovus Energy has agreed to purchase MEG Energy. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Home-purchase loan applications dropped from 7.9 million to 6.5 million nationwide - Anat Nusinovich.
data.description: The Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA) recently reported a significant decrease in housing demand across the country. The data shows a notable drop in the number of home-purchase loan applications, decreasing from 7.9 million to 6.5 million. This decline in applications suggests a weakening interest in homeownership among individuals.

data.title: US and China agree to temporary tariff reduction in May, existing duties still in place, raising hopes for trade truce.
data.description: In May, the United States decided to reduce tariffs on Chinese imports temporarily while keeping existing duties unchanged.

data.title: ICP price at $5.04 near key support of $4.84, RSI 44.72. Technicals signal ongoing consolidation phase.
data.description: The current trading price of ICP is $5.04 with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 44.72. It is approaching a crucial support level at $4.84, indicating a continued consolidation phase based on technical indicators.

data.title: IperionX approved for tax-exempt private activity bond issuance of $150 million.
data.description: A recent report states that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, marking a significant increase from the 3.5% contraction seen in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. This growth is driven by strong performances in major economies, with the U.S. economy expected to expand by 6.2%, China by 8.4%, and the Eurozone by 4.3%. However, disparities remain as emerging markets and developing economies are projected to grow by only 4.6%. Inflation rates are also expected to rise, with the global average predicted to be around 3.8%. Despite these positive outlooks, uncertainties still linger due to factors like vaccine distribution, new COVID-19 variants, and geopolitical tensions. Experts are closely monitoring the situation to assess any potential risks to the projected growth.

data.title: US regulator allows Americans to trade on foreign crypto exchanges as part of “crypto sprint” reform, following Trump admin proposals.
data.description: As part of the "crypto sprint" initiative by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, regulations are being revamped in response to proposals from the previous administration. This change aims to address key issues in crypto trading oversight.
Fannie and Freddie face financial challenges, impacting the housing market.

data.title: Aledade adds 2 health experts to Board of Directors - Shawn Guertin & Joneigh S. Khaldun, M.D. - based in Bethesda, Maryland.
data.description: In Bethesda, Maryland, Aledade revealed the addition of two prominent figures in the healthcare industry to its Board of Directors. Shawn Guertin and Joneigh S. Khaldun, M.D., have been appointed to the board, bringing valuable expertise to the company's leadership team.
